pipelines.feature_name: Rename 'Class is Complete' banner to 'Course is Complete' / 'Organization Course is Complete'
pipelines.outcome_reason: User requested rollback after successful implementation (changes stashed for future use)
lessons: Learn uses locale-layered resource bundles — en_US_k12 overrides en_US defaults. When renaming 'Class' to 'Course', only K12 locale needed changes because others already used 'Course'.
BreadcrumbBarRenderer.java already handles course vs org distinction via course.isCourse()/course.isOrganization() — NO Java code changes needed for conditional banner text.
For cosmetic text changes in Learn Original: check properties files in workspace/base/base-locale/<locale>/messages/ — not Java code.
Quick-fix mode would have been sufficient for this change in hindsight (2 files, text-only, zero logic change). Consider downgrading full-feature to quick-fix when discovery reveals trivial scope.
The 'Organization Course is Complete' phrasing is unusual — always flag wording concerns to product team before implementing.
pipelines.key_discovery: The architecture cleanly separates display text (properties) from rendering logic (Java). Banner conditional logic is in BreadcrumbBarRenderer.java (L509-528).

pipelines.feature_name: Add 'Last Access' column to course roster page
pipelines.outcome_reason: Core pipeline succeeded but 2 bugs found during live testing required post-pipeline fixes
patterns_used: Foundation 12-column grid layout for roster list view
AngularJS date filter for date formatting
ng-if conditional rendering for null handling

issues_found.description: Template bound to wrong property path: membership.lastAccessDate (empty) instead of membership.user.lastLoginDate (populated)
issues_found.root_cause: Discovery identified both lastAccessDate (top-level) and user.lastLoginDate (nested). Implementation used wrong path. The nested user object field was the one actually populated.

issues_found.description: bb-date directive rendered literal text instead of formatted date — directive module not registered in roster component
issues_found.root_cause: AngularJS requires directive modules to be explicitly declared in component module dependencies. The bb-date directive (ultra.directives.bbDate) was not in course-roster.ts module array.
issues_found.final_fix: Replaced bb-date directive with built-in AngularJS date filter ({{ expr | date:'short' }}) which requires no module imports

lessons: CRITICAL: In Ultra's AngularJS architecture, custom directives (bb-date, bb-datetime) only work if their module is imported in the component's module definition. Always verify directive availability before using one.
SAFER APPROACH: Use AngularJS built-in filters ({{ expr | date:'short' }}) instead of custom directives for simple date formatting. They always work without module imports.

API response field paths: When the API returns nested objects (e.g., user.lastLoginDate inside membership), verify the EXACT property path in the Angular model. The frontend model may flatten or nest differently than expected.

Discovery identified 'lastAccessDate' (course-level) vs 'user.lastLoginDate' (system-level) distinction, but the wrong one was used in implementation. When two similar fields exist, always verify which one is actually populated by checking the Network tab.
Parallel discovery saved significant time by revealing zero backend work was needed — what was scoped as 7/10 cross-repo complexity became a 3/10 frontend-only change.
The roster module (course-roster.ts) has a limited set of directive dependencies. Any new directive usage requires adding to the module array.
User.lastLoginDate comes from the embedded user object in the memberships API response. The frontend model exposes it as membership.user.lastLoginDate (not membership.lastLoginDate).
pipelines.key_discovery: The membership API already returns lastLoginDate in the embedded user object. The frontend model already deserializes it. Zero backend changes and zero new API fields needed — purely a UI column addition.
clarifications_needed: System login vs course access: Product clarification resolved that lastLoginDate (system login) was acceptable
Date format: User requested date + time (short format), not date-only
